title: The button that was never found

mcq.question_md:
This script sits in `<head>`, and the console shows *"Cannot read properties of null (reading 'addEventListener')"*:

```html
<head>
  <script>
    const btn = document.querySelector("#markBtn");
    btn.addEventListener("click", markPresent);
  </script>
</head>
<body>
  <button id="markBtn">Mark present</button>
</body>
```

What's actually wrong?
options: The script ran before the button existed in the DOM, so querySelector returned null
querySelector can't find elements by id — only getElementById can
addEventListener doesn't work on button elements
The function markPresent must be defined inside the button's onclick attribute
mcq.explanation_md: The browser reads the page top to bottom. A script in `<head>` runs *before* `<body>` is parsed, so at that moment `#markBtn` doesn't exist and `querySelector` returns `null` — and calling anything on `null` throws. Fix: move the script to the end of `<body>` or add `defer` to the script tag. The `getElementById` option is the tempting distractor — `querySelector("#markBtn")` handles id selectors perfectly well; the selector isn't the problem, the *timing* is. Buttons absolutely support `addEventListener`, and `onclick` attributes are the older style, not a requirement.
